# Yassir

Yassir is an open-source AI research agent for halal finance, Islamic finance,
and Shariah-compliant investing. It is CLI-first, self-hostable, written in
TypeScript/Bun, and includes a Hono API plus React companion web UI.

Canonical repository: https://github.com/goww7/yassir-oss
License: Apache-2.0
Primary data partner: HalalTerminal API, https://api.halalterminal.com

## What Yassir Does

- Shariah stock and ETF screening
- Portfolio compliance audits
- Dividend purification context
- Halal replacement idea generation
- SEC filing and company facts research
- Market quotes, OHLC history, news, and asset profiles
- Agentic research workflows using tool calls and sourced synthesis

## Quickstart

```bash
git clone https://github.com/goww7/yassir-oss.git
cd yassir-oss
bun install
cp env.example .env
```

Required keys:

```env
OPENAI_API_KEY=...
HALAL_TERMINAL_API_KEY=...
```

Run the CLI:

```bash
bun dev
```

Run the web companion:

```bash
bun run web:install
bun run web:dev
bun run web:ui
```

## Important Files

- `README.md`: human project overview and setup
- `AGENTS.md`: coding-agent instructions for this repository
- `docs/examples.md`: user prompts and workflow examples
- `src/agent/`: ReAct loop, prompts, scratchpad, progress synthesis
- `src/tools/finance/`: HalalTerminal-backed finance and Shariah tools
- `src/integrations/halalterminal/`: HalalTerminal API boundary
- `src/tools/open-data/`: SEC EDGAR company facts and submissions
- `src/web/`: Hono API server
- `web/`: React companion app

## Common User Prompts

- "Is AAPL Shariah-compliant? Show the full screening breakdown."
- "/audit AAPL MSFT SPUS"
- "/purification AAPL MSFT"
- "/ideas replace QQQ with halal alternatives"
- "Summarize Tesla's latest 10-K risk factors."

## Development Commands

```bash
bun run typecheck
bun test
bun run web:install
bun run web:build
```

## Boundaries

Yassir is the open-source agent and application layer. HalalTerminal provides
the primary Shariah and halal-finance data layer. Preserve HalalTerminal
attribution in `NOTICE`, README references, and user-facing powered-by links.
